marketState: neutral with a slight bearish tilt — price sits at the upper edge of fair value while storage and seasonality skew lower
summary: Front-month NG is trading at $3.214, about 65% up inside its $2.429-$3.429 fair-value band (fair $2.929, 'SLIGHTLY OVER'), and that fair-value estimate climbs toward $3.05-$3.07 over the next 2-3 weeks, meaning today's premium naturally shrinks even without a selloff. Seasonality shows a soft downward skew (only 31% of years were higher 4 weeks out, typical move -3.1%) but the dashboard itself flags this reliability as 'Low' (50pp spread), so it's a tiebreaker, not a conviction driver. Storage is +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bigger-than-forecast bearish build, reinforcing an ample-supply backdrop. Near-term weather demand is still 12% above normal (rangePos 0.81) which is near-term supportive, but the 24h trend is fading (-11.1), and multi-timeframe technicals lean bearish (1d/1w downtrend, 50<200 EMA) without being oversold. Managed money is net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, so positioning is moderately bearish without being crowded enough to fuel a squeeze either way.

longTerm.rationale: Price is at the rich end of the fair-value band while the band's fair midpoint itself is only forecast to drift up to ~$3.05-3.07 over 3 weeks, seasonality skews down over a 4-week window (31% higher, typical -3.1%), and storage is +175 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish surprise build — all pointing to gradual mean reversion toward fair value.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.43 (top of the current fair-value band) or a run of bullish storage surprises/deficits vs 5y would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5% account risk (mixed signals, keep size small)
shortTerm.rationale: Front is priced with a $0.285 premium to today's fair value (converging to $0.204 by day 7), technicals lean bearish on the 15m/1h/1d timeframes with EMA 50<200, and the front slightly backwardates to Sep (-0.075) — but 1-7D weather demand is still 12% above normal, so this is a modest fade, not a high-conviction breakdown.
shortTerm.invalidation: A push back toward $3.35-3.40 without technical confirmation, or a re-acceleration in 1-7D weather demand (rangePos pushing toward/above 1.0), would invalidate this fade.
